en.extract:
The Palau fruit dove (Ptilinopus pelewensis) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is endemic to Palau and it is also Palau's national bird.


Description
The Palau fruit dove is 23 to 25 cm (9.1–9.8 in) long and weighs around 93 g (3.3 oz). The species has a grey head, neck and breast with a purple cap and yellow line. There is an orange band across the lower breast and belly and olive-green back, wings and tail. Both sexes are alike. Juvenile birds resemble the adults but lack the purple cap, the green upperparts are lined with yellow and the belly is yellow.


Distribution and habitat
The Palau fruit dove is endemic to Palau, where it is common in all habitats across the island. The species declined due to hunting prior to 1945, but has rebounded with protection and is now common.


References

The '''Palau fruit dove''' ('''''Ptilinopus pelewensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Palau]] and it is also Palau's [[List of national birds|national bird]].<ref>{{cite book|last1=Minahan|first1=James|title=The Complete Guide to National Symbols and Emblems [2 Volumes] |date=2009 |publisher=ABC-CLIO |location=Santa Barbara |isbn=978-0-313-34497-8 |page=146 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=jfrWCQAAQBAJ&q=national+bird+of+palau&pg=PA147}}</ref>

==Description==
The Palau fruit dove is {{convert|23|to(-)|25|cm|in|abbr=on}} long and weighs around {{convert|93|g|abbr=on}}. The species has a grey head, neck and breast with a purple cap and yellow line. There is an orange band across the lower breast and belly and olive-green back, wings and tail. Both sexes are alike. Juvenile birds resemble the adults but lack the purple cap, the green upperparts are lined with yellow and the belly is yellow.<ref name="hbw">{{cite web |last1=Baptista|first1=L.|last2=Trail|first2=P.|last3=Horblit|first3=H.|last4=Boesman|first4=P.|editor1-last=del Hoyo |editor1-first=Josep |editor2-last=Elliott |editor2-first=Andrew |editor3-last=Sargatal |editor3-first=Jordi |editor4-last=Christie |editor4-first=David A. |editor5-last=de Juana |editor5-first=Eduardo |year=2017 |title=Palau Fruit-dove (''Ptilinopus pelewensis'')|work=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive |url=http://www.hbw.com/species/palau-fruit-dove-ptilinopus-pelewensis |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona, Spain |access-date=12 July 2017 |url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
The Palau fruit dove is endemic to [[Palau]], where it is common in all habitats across the island.<ref name="hbw"/> The species declined due to hunting prior to 1945, but has rebounded with protection and is now common.<ref name="Pratt">{{cite journal|last1=Pratt|first1=H. Douglas|last2=Engbring|first2=John|last3=Bruner|first3=Phillip L.|last4=Berrett|first4=Delwyn G.|title=Notes on the Taxonomy, Natural History, and Status of the Resident Birds of Palau|journal=The Condor|date=May 1980|volume=82|issue=2|page=117|doi=10.2307/1367460|jstor=1367460|url=http://hdouglaspratt.com/journal_articles/Pratt%20et%20al%201980%20Palau.pdf}}</ref>

zh.extract:
帕劳果鸠（台湾作帛琉果鴿，学名：Ptilinopus pelewensis），是鸽形目鸠鸽科的一种鸟类。它們是帛琉的特有種，也是當地的國鳥。


特征
帕劳果鸠体重约93克，翼长约12.78厘米，喙宽度约4.3毫米，喙厚度约4.1毫米，嘴峰长约18.2毫米，跗蹠长约23.8毫米，尾长约66.2毫米。
帕劳果鸠是留鸟，栖息在森林，它们是植食性动物，主要以植物的果实为食。它们分布在帕劳群岛。
